_meta.description: Aqueduct structured evidence base — peer-reviewed citations with effect sizes, certainty ratings, and clinical significance thresholds

hierarchy.HIGH: Systematic review or meta-analysis with GRADE HIGH certainty; pooled estimate from ≥3 RCTs
hierarchy.MODERATE: RCT or well-designed observational study; meta-analysis with moderate heterogeneity
hierarchy.LOW: Single observational study; pilot data; difference-in-differences without randomization
hierarchy.VERY_LOW: Expert opinion; industry data not peer-reviewed; mechanistic reasoning only

nemt_studies.citation: Shekelle PG, Begashaw MM, Miake-Lye IM, et al. BMC Public Health. 2022;22:799. PMID:35449011
nemt_studies.title: Effect of interventions for non-emergent medical transportation: a systematic review and meta-analysis
nemt_studies.population: 12 studies (3 RCTs), van/taxi/rideshare, Medicaid and LMI populations
nemt_studies.clinical_significance: HIGH — appointment adherence is directly actionable
nemt_studies.outcomes_evidence: LOW — insufficient data for cost, utilization, or health outcomes conclusions
nemt_studies.key_limitation: Publication bias likely; all studies in US ambulatory setting

nemt_studies.citation: Chaiyachati KH et al. JAMA Internal Medicine. 2018;178(3):383-389
nemt_studies.title: Association of rideshare-based transportation services and missed primary care appointments: a clinical trial
nemt_studies.finding: Universal untargeted rideshare: NO reduction in no-shows
nemt_studies.population: 786 Medicaid beneficiaries, West Philadelphia, complimentary Lyft
nemt_studies.key_lesson: Only 26% used rides. Universal programs fail because most patients at any given visit do NOT have transportation as their primary barrier.

nemt_studies.citation: Chaiyachati KH et al. J General Internal Medicine. 2018;33(6):863-868
nemt_studies.title: Rideshare-based medical transportation for Medicaid patients and primary care show rates
nemt_studies.finding: 14 percentage-point improvement in show rates (targeted practice vs. control)
nemt_studies.reconciliation: With RCT: targeted programs (practice-level, not individual-level) outperform universal offers

nemt_studies.citation: Hitch Health / Lyft Healthcare / Hennepin Healthcare. 2018 (industry report, not peer-reviewed)
nemt_studies.title: SMS-triggered targeted NEMT for prior no-show patients
nemt_studies.population: Patients with prior no-show history, automated SMS + ride offer
nemt_studies.key_lesson: Targeting method: automated identification of prior no-shows is sufficient and scalable. No manual screening required.
nemt_studies.caveat: Industry data — not peer-reviewed. Treat as directional, not definitive.

nemt_studies.citation: Berkowitz SA et al. J Primary Care & Community Health. 2025. PMID cited as PMC11840852
nemt_studies.title: Transportation barriers and glycemic and blood pressure control among adults with type 2 diabetes
nemt_studies.method: TMLE (targeted minimum loss-based estimation) — rigorous causal inference
nemt_studies.finding: Transportation barrier removal: -0.09% HbA1c; -0.6 mmHg SBP
nemt_studies.clinical_significance: BELOW THRESHOLD — 0.5% HbA1c is minimum clinically significant difference. Effect is real but small when transport is addressed in isolation.
nemt_studies.key_lesson: NEMT alone is insufficient. Must be embedded in multi-component care model (CCM, TCM, CHW) to achieve clinical significance.

nemt_studies.citation: Smith et al. Health Services Research. 2022;57(3):472-481
nemt_studies.title: Public transit expansion and healthcare no-show rates
nemt_studies.finding: 0.5pp no-show reduction overall; 1.6pp among Medicaid patients
nemt_studies.key_lesson: Effects concentrate in populations with genuine access barriers — supports targeting approach

nemt_studies.citation: Berkowitz SA et al. Health Affairs. 2022;41(3):406-413
nemt_studies.title: Evaluation of a Medicare ACO NEMT program
nemt_studies.finding: Increased outpatient visits (+9.2/person/year) but NO reduction in admissions or ED visits. Program NOT cost-saving.
nemt_studies.critical_lesson: NEMT alone, without accompanying CCM/TCM and care management, does not reduce total cost of care. It increases utilization without downstream benefits if care coordination is absent.
nemt_studies.implication_for_platform: NEMT must be paired with CCM/TCM to generate cost savings. This is why Layer 2 (CCM/TCM) is critical to the ROI case.

readmission_studies.citation: Balasubramanian I, Andres EB, Malhotra C. JAMA Network Open. 2025;8(11):e2541272. PMC12587199
readmission_studies.title: Outpatient follow-up and 30-day readmissions: systematic review and meta-analysis
readmission_studies.finding: 30-day outpatient follow-up: 32% reduction in all-cause readmissions
readmission_studies.nemt_connection: NEMT enables post-discharge follow-up. If transport barrier prevents follow-up, readmission risk rises substantially. This is the mechanistic bridge.

ccm_tcm_studies.citation: Ouayogodé et al. Health Services Research. 2024. PMC11578547

ccm_tcm_studies.finding: TCM decreased healthcare spending by 11%; $1.66B potential savings if all eligible received TCM. Only 17.9% of eligible beneficiaries received TCM in 2019.
ccm_tcm_studies.implication: Capture opportunity: 82.1% of eligible Medicare patients are not receiving TCM billing. NEMT enables the visits that generate the billing.

99491.desc: CCM physician/QHP first 30min — cannot be incident-to

99495.desc: TCM moderate MDM F2F within 14 days

99496.desc: TCM high MDM F2F within 7 days

ccm_tcm_studies.non_duplication: CCM and TCM can be billed in same calendar month — TCM 30-day period must end; ≥20min qualifying CCM services must follow; time cannot be double-counted

workforce_studies.citation: NSI Nursing Solutions Inc. 2025 NSI National Health Care Retention & RN Staffing Report
workforce_studies.finding: Average RN turnover cost: $61,110. One percentage point change in hospital turnover = $380,600/year for average hospital.

workforce_studies.citation: Medical Group Management Association. Practice Operations Survey 2024.
workforce_studies.finding: $150,000/year average revenue loss per physician from no-shows
workforce_studies.relevant_for: Layer 0 FFS baseline and Layer 4 provider idle time

measure_001.name: Diabetes HbA1c Poor Control >9% (INVERSE)
measure_001.transportation_sensitivity: EXTREME — missing visit = missing HbA1c test = automatically counted as poorly controlled
measure_001.benchmark: Top decile ≤10% of patients with poor control
measure_001.nemt_connection: Every NEMT-enabled visit for a diabetic patient directly improves numerator

measure_236.name: Controlling High Blood Pressure
measure_236.transportation_sensitivity: HIGH — requires in-person BP measurement; no recorded BP = uncontrolled
measure_236.structure: Flat — each 10pp improvement = one decile improvement

measure_113.name: Colorectal Cancer Screening
measure_113.transportation_sensitivity: MODERATE — colonoscopy requires driver for post-sedation; NEMT doubles the required trips
